The callback smell sensor already computes exactly the inputs (cc, captured, captured-mutable, await-count). Prevention, not a refactor → autonomous ratchet.

The T0 mass here is marginal vs the file's real cost, which is startServer/buildAsApp (chosen as A2). T0 on this file is the streetlight bias — mechanical cc-6/7 clearance that leaves the mass (attention=70850) untouched. Do the A2 first; T0 opportunistically after.
Same streetlight trap — T0 clears the 41.8%-by-count/16.3%-by-mass tail while handleMsg (cc=194) sits untouched. Superseded by rt-index-handlemsg-a2-decomplect.

records.js's mass is in queryRecords (cc=99, T2-property) and the cross-binding aggregators (cc=73 each, T2-async) — property/async tiers needing judged proof, not an exact-move. The exact-move packet addresses a marginal slice; route the real mass to A2 in a later slice. Deferred, not rejected.

The prior runtime-scheduler-t0 attempt REVERTED at $2.93 with a REJECT judge (COST-ACTUALS). Lower attention (runtime/scheduler.ts:attention=1260) than the chosen A2 targets and a known-hard/expensive surface — defer until the handleMsg/startServer A2 method is proven cheaper.
owner_ratify gate + a delete against a claims ledger I did not author; the guidance is to surface, not proceed. Belongs to the deterministic floor / owner, not the agenda-setter.

db.js mass is in the .transaction callbacks (cc=52/39, T2-property, DDL markers CREATE/DROP TABLE) — T2-in-disguise (T1c), not a T0. A T0 land here would move a metric without touching the transaction-seam mass. Route to evidence-gen + judged later.
